title: Admin SSR memory leak + cheap healthcheck

tasks.title: Verify TanStack Start server-route primitive for admin version
tasks.description:
Investigate and document which mechanism the installed TanStack Start version provides for raw (non-SSR) HTTP handlers:
- File: apps/admin/package.json — read the exact @tanstack/react-start version
- Check TanStack Start docs / node_modules for 'createAPIRoute', 'serverRoute', or equivalent API-route primitive that returns a raw Response without triggering React SSR
- Determine if a file at apps/admin/src/routes/healthz.ts (or healthz.tsx) with an export named Route (API route form) would be picked up by the router without rendering the React root
- If the primitive exists: document the exact import path and signature needed for T-002
- If no first-class primitive: identify the fallback (Nitro route in apps/admin/src/routes/api/ or middleware early-return for /healthz)
- Write findings as a one-paragraph comment block at the top of apps/admin/src/routes/healthz.ts (create the file stub even if empty)

Files affected:
- apps/admin/package.json (read only)
- apps/admin/src/routes/healthz.ts (create stub with findings comment)

Test requirements: none — this is an investigation/decision task. Output is the stub file with a findings comment and a note in the PR description indicating which mechanism was chosen.

subtasks.title: Read @tanstack/react-start version from package.json
Check node_modules or docs for API-route / server-route primitive
Create apps/admin/src/routes/healthz.ts stub with findings comment

tasks.title: Implement /healthz server route returning 200 without React SSR
tasks.description:
Implement the cheap healthcheck endpoint in apps/admin/src/routes/healthz.ts using the mechanism confirmed in T-001:
- Route MUST handle GET /healthz and return HTTP 200 with body { "status": "ok" } and Content-Type: application/json
- Route MUST NOT invoke the React root component, construct a QueryClient, or construct a QZPayBilling instance
- If using TanStack Start API route: export a Route using createServerFn or the equivalent non-SSR handler; wrap in try/catch and return 500 if anything throws unexpectedly
- If using Nitro route fallback: place handler in apps/admin/src/routes/api/healthz.ts or equivalent Nitro-recognized path, export default event handler returning the JSON response
- Add a JSDoc block explaining the route purpose and the AC it satisfies (AC-1.1, AC-1.2)
- The endpoint must respond in < 50 ms (no DB calls, no external calls)

Files affected:
- apps/admin/src/routes/healthz.ts (or healthz.tsx)

Test requirements:
- Unit test in apps/admin/test/lib/healthz.test.ts:
  - 'GET /healthz returns 200 and {status: ok} body'
  - 'GET /healthz does not construct QZPayBilling' (mock createQZPayBilling, assert not called)
  - Test the route handler function in isolation (no need for a full server; call the exported handler directly or via supertest if available)
- These tests serve as the regression baseline for AC-1.1 and AC-1.2

subtasks.title: Implement route handler with JSON 200 response
Confirm no SSR / no QZPayBilling construction path
Add JSDoc + AC references
Write unit test in apps/admin/test/lib/healthz.test.ts

tasks.title: Repoint Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK to /healthz
tasks.description:
Update apps/admin/Dockerfile to point the container healthcheck at /healthz instead of /:
- Currently there is no explicit HEALTHCHECK instruction in the Dockerfile (Coolify defines it externally). Add an explicit HEALTHCHECK instruction in the runner stage:
  HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=15s --retries=3 CMD curl -f http://localhost:3000/healthz || exit 1
- Verify curl is already installed (it is — 'apk add --no-cache curl' is present in the runner stage)
- Add a comment above the HEALTHCHECK line referencing SPEC-209 and explaining that / triggers full React SSR
- Note in a PR comment that the Coolify resource UI healthcheck Test field may also need to be updated from 'curl -f http://localhost:3000/' to 'curl -f http://localhost:3000/healthz' — this is an operator step (AC-1.3)

Files affected:
- apps/admin/Dockerfile

Test requirements:
- This change cannot be unit-tested; verification is:
  - Run 'docker build' locally to confirm the image builds (typecheck gate)
  - The integration test in T-005 covers the /healthz endpoint behavior
  - PR description must include the operator checklist item for Coolify UI update

subtasks.title: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction to Dockerfile runner stage
Add explanatory comment referencing SPEC-209
Note Coolify UI operator step in PR description

tasks.title: Define heap-snapshot procedure and RSS-band baseline
tasks.description:
Produce a documented, executable procedure for confirming the memory leak root cause before the fix. This is an investigation task — output is a markdown document, not code:

- File: apps/admin/docs/spec-209-heap-snapshot-procedure.md (create if docs/ does not exist)
- Document the staging trigger mechanism: pick ONE of:
  a) node --heapsnapshot-signal=SIGUSR2 flag added to CMD in Dockerfile (dev/staging only)
  b) A staging-only admin route that calls v8.writeHeapSnapshot() behind AUTH_CHECK (preferred for safety)
- RSS sampling script: document the command to poll RSS every 30 s for N minutes while a probe loop hits /healthz (before and after), e.g.:
    while true; do docker stats --no-stream --format '{{.MemUsage}}' <container_id>; sleep 30; done
- Define the RSS acceptance band for AC-2.3: based on the observed ~986 MB / 48 h growth, a 'good' run should hold steady within ±50 MB over a 30-minute probe window. Document this as the concrete pass/fail criterion.
- Document the QZPayBilling initialized log count check: before fix, count should correlate with probe count; after fix, count should be 0 per probe.
- This document becomes the reference for the manual staging smoke (AC-2.1, AC-2.3)

Files affected:
- apps/admin/docs/spec-209-heap-snapshot-procedure.md (new)

Test requirements: none — documentation task. The procedure is validated during the manual staging smoke in T-012.

subtasks.title: Choose and document the heap-snapshot trigger mechanism
Write RSS polling script and 30-min window procedure
Define RSS acceptance band (±50 MB over 30 min)
Document QZPayBilling log count check method

tasks.title: Write integration test for /healthz response contract
tasks.description:
Write an integration-level test that calls the /healthz endpoint and asserts the full response contract (AC-1.1, AC-1.2):

- File: apps/admin/test/lib/healthz.test.ts (this file is already created in T-002 for unit tests; extend it here with integration-style assertions, or split into a separate file apps/admin/test/integration/healthz.test.ts)
- Test cases:
  1. 'returns HTTP 200 for GET /healthz'
  2. 'response body is {status: "ok"} (valid JSON)'
  3. 'response Content-Type includes application/json'
  4. 'no QZPayBilling instance is constructed on probe' — mock/spy createQZPayBilling from @qazuor/qzpay-core and assert it was not called when the healthz handler runs
  5. 'response time < 50ms' (optional, as a sanity guard — use performance.now() before/after calling the handler in-process)
- Use the vitest environment (jsdom or node — use node environment for this test file since it tests a server handler, not DOM)
- If the handler can be called directly (exported function), call it directly. If it requires a running Nitro server, use msw or undici MockAgent to intercept.

Files affected:
- apps/admin/test/lib/healthz.test.ts (extend) or apps/admin/test/integration/healthz.test.ts (new)

Note: these tests must pass before T-011 (regression tests) can validate the fixed behavior.

subtasks.title: Add/extend healthz test file with integration assertions
Mock createQZPayBilling and assert not-called on probe
Assert response status, body shape, and Content-Type

tasks.title: Write regression test reproducing SSR billing construction bug
tasks.description:
Write a test that reproduces the original bug (QZPayBilling constructed on every SSR render) BEFORE the fix is applied, then confirm it passes after the fix. This is the pre-fix regression test that must fail on the unmodified __root.tsx:

- File: apps/admin/test/regression/ssr-billing-construction.test.tsx (new)
- Test setup:
  - Mock createQZPayBilling from @qazuor/qzpay-core with vi.mock (spy to count calls)
  - Mock useSession from @/lib/auth-client (return { data: null })
  - Render RootDocument (extract and export if not already exported from __root.tsx) in a Node/jsdom environment simulating N=5 server-side renders (renderToString or @testing-library/react render in server mode)
  - Each render MUST produce a fresh component instance (unmount between renders to simulate SSR per-request)
- Assertions (this test should FAIL on unmodified __root.tsx, PASS after T-009 fix):
  1. 'createQZPayBilling is called at most once across N SSR renders' — i.e., not called on every render
  2. 'QZPayBilling initialized log is not emitted on each SSR render' (spy on logger/console.log for the 'QZPayBilling initialized' message)
- Mark the test with a comment: 'Regression test for SPEC-209 AC-2.2 — must fail before fix, pass after fix (T-009)'

Files affected:
- apps/admin/test/regression/ssr-billing-construction.test.tsx (new)
- apps/admin/src/routes/__root.tsx may need to export RootDocument for testability

Note: this task depends on T-004 (procedure) to understand what to measure, but the test itself is independent code. It also blocks T-009 (the fix) because you write the test first.

subtasks.title: Export RootDocument from __root.tsx (if needed) for testability
Create test file with mock setup for createQZPayBilling and useSession
Write N-render loop asserting call count ≤ 1
Assert no 'QZPayBilling initialized' log per-render

tasks.title: Write static-analysis guard test for admin root SSR-unsafe construction
tasks.description:
Write a static-analysis guard test that fails if createQZPayBilling or new QueryClient appears in __root.tsx in a server-executed code path without a client-only guard (AC-3.1, AC-3.2):

- File: apps/admin/test/guard/ssr-root-guard.test.ts (new)
- Approach: read apps/admin/src/routes/__root.tsx as text (fs.readFileSync), then:
  1. Assert that any occurrence of 'createQZPayBilling(' is preceded within the same code block by a client-only guard: typeof window !== 'undefined', useEffect, or isomorphicLayoutEffect — or appears inside a function/component whose name indicates client-only (e.g. inside useEffect callback body)
  2. Assert that any occurrence of 'new QueryClient(' is similarly guarded OR is inside a useState initializer that has a corresponding isServer check
  3. The simplest reliable check: assert that 'createQZPayBilling(' does NOT appear outside of a useEffect body in the file. A regex-based check is acceptable for this guard.
- The test SHOULD FAIL on the current (unfixed) __root.tsx before T-009 is applied, and PASS after.
- Add a descriptive comment explaining WHY this guard exists and which incident it prevents.

Files affected:
- apps/admin/test/guard/ssr-root-guard.test.ts (new)

Test requirements: this IS the test. CI must run it on every PR (vitest picks it up via the existing 'test/**/*.test.ts' glob).

subtasks.title: Create guard test reading __root.tsx as text
Assert createQZPayBilling only in client-guarded context
Add descriptive comment about the guard purpose

tasks.title: Write static-analysis guard test for web root SSR-unsafe construction
tasks.description:
Extend the SSR guard to cover apps/web to prevent the pattern from being introduced there (AC-3.1 second half):

- File: apps/admin/test/guard/ssr-root-guard.test.ts (extend the file from T-007, or create apps/web/test/guard/ssr-root-guard.test.ts if web has its own test runner)
- Determine the web root file: the web app uses Astro, so there is no __root.tsx. The relevant entry is apps/web/src/layouts/ (main layout) and apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/ root pages. Read those files and assert:
  1. No occurrence of 'createQZPayBilling(' in any Astro layout/page file that is server-rendered
  2. No occurrence of 'new QueryClient(' outside of a client:* directive context
- If the web root is clean today (as per spec — 'apps/web does NOT build a QueryClient/billing in its root'), the test acts as a pinning assertion that WOULD fail if someone adds it later.
- Add a comment: 'Pin test — web root is currently clean. Fails if createQZPayBilling / new QueryClient introduced outside client:* context.'

Files affected:
- apps/admin/test/guard/ssr-root-guard.test.ts (extend) OR apps/web/test/guard/ (new)
- Must determine which vitest config covers web — if web uses a separate vitest.config.ts, place the test there

Test requirements: this IS the test. Part of CI suite.

subtasks.title: Identify the web root/layout files to scan
Extend or create guard test asserting web root is clean
Verify the test is picked up by the appropriate vitest config

tasks.title: Smoke-test admin billing pages after client-only billing fix
tasks.description:
Manually verify that client-side billing still works correctly after the client-only billing guard change (risk: client-only billing breaks an SSR-time billing read, per spec risk table):

- This is a manual smoke task (documented procedure), not a code task.
- Smoke steps to execute locally or on staging:
  1. Load the admin billing section (e.g. /billing/plans, /billing/subscriptions)
  2. Verify the QZPayProvider billing context is accessible in client-side React (useQZPay hook returns a non-null billing instance)
  3. Verify plan list loads correctly (billing HTTP adapter resolves)
  4. Check browser console — no 'billing is null' or 'QZPayProvider requires billing' errors
  5. Trigger a billing operation if safe (e.g. view plan details, NOT a real payment)
- Document the smoke results in the PR description with: environment (local/staging), date, result (PASS/FAIL), any notes
- If billing is broken client-side: this task BLOCKS the PR — fix must be revised in T-009

Files affected: none (manual smoke task)

Test requirements:
- Automated billing access test already exists at apps/admin/test/lib/billing-access.test.ts — run it and confirm it passes
- This smoke is the final gate before T-011 (integration test completion) and T-012 (staging validation)

subtasks.title: Run existing billing-access.test.ts and confirm green
Load admin billing pages locally / on staging
Verify QZPayProvider client-side context is non-null
Document smoke results in PR description

tasks.title: Write post-fix integration test asserting no billing initialization on SSR
tasks.description:
Write (or extend) the integration test that confirms the full AC-2.2 fix is in place: after the T-009 change, the admin SSR path must not construct QZPayBilling (AC-2.2):

- File: apps/admin/test/regression/ssr-billing-construction.test.tsx (from T-006 — this task adds post-fix assertions in the same file)
- Add a test section: 'after fix: billing is not constructed on server render'
  - Same setup as T-006: mock createQZPayBilling, render RootDocument N=5 times (server-side)
  - Assert: createQZPayBilling call count is 0 across all server renders
  - Assert: 'QZPayBilling initialized' log NOT emitted
- Also add: 'billing initializes exactly once on client mount'
  - Use jsdom environment (simulates browser)
  - Render RootDocument, wait for useEffect to fire
  - Assert: createQZPayBilling called exactly 1 time
- Link assertions to AC-2.2 in comments

Files affected:
- apps/admin/test/regression/ssr-billing-construction.test.tsx (extend)

This test must be green before T-012 (staging validation) can proceed.

subtasks.title: Add post-fix server-render assertions (call count = 0)

tasks.title: Execute staging heap-snapshot and RSS-band validation
tasks.description:
Execute the procedure defined in T-004 on the staging admin container and record before/after results (AC-2.1, AC-2.3). This is a manual staging task:

- Pre-fix baseline (before deploying T-009):
  1. Deploy staging with only T-001/T-002/T-003 applied (healthcheck fix only)
  2. Run the RSS polling script from T-004 for 30 minutes with /healthz probe loop
  3. Count 'QZPayBilling initialized' log entries (should be 0 since /healthz bypasses SSR)
  4. Record baseline RSS (expected: stable since probes now hit /healthz)
- Post-fix validation (after deploying T-009):
  1. Deploy staging with all SPEC-209 changes
  2. Run the RSS polling script again for 30 minutes with mixed probe traffic (/ and /healthz)
  3. Count 'QZPayBilling initialized' entries for / traffic (should decrease significantly or become 0)
  4. Confirm RSS stays within the ±50 MB band defined in T-004
- Trigger and analyze heap snapshot if RSS still grows (follow T-004 procedure)
- Document findings in apps/admin/docs/spec-209-heap-snapshot-procedure.md (results section)
- AC-2.3 pass criterion: RSS within ±50 MB over 30 min with no monotonic trend

Files affected:
- apps/admin/docs/spec-209-heap-snapshot-procedure.md (append results section)

This is a mandatory gate for marking SPEC-209 complete.

subtasks.title: Deploy staging with healthcheck fix only
Execute pre-fix RSS baseline (30-min probe window)
Deploy staging with full SPEC-209 fix
Execute post-fix RSS validation and QZPayBilling log count check
Trigger heap snapshot if RSS still grows
Record results in procedure document

tasks.title: Update apps/admin/CLAUDE.md with healthcheck contract and memory-leak fix
tasks.description:
Document the SPEC-209 changes in the admin app's CLAUDE.md so future maintainers understand the healthcheck architecture and the SSR-safety requirement (AC-3.2 — guard test must be documented):

- Add a section 'Healthcheck' under the architecture section:
  - /healthz is a cheap endpoint (no SSR, no QZPayBilling) — used by Coolify / Docker HEALTHCHECK
  - Never add server-side business logic to the healthcheck handler
  - The Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK and Coolify resource UI Test field must both point to /healthz
- Add a section 'SSR Safety Rules':
  - createQZPayBilling MUST NOT be called in any server-executed code path (useEffect only)
  - Guard test: apps/admin/test/guard/ssr-root-guard.test.ts — explain what it checks
  - QueryClient per-request is intentional (TanStack SSR pattern) — do NOT move it client-only
- Link to apps/admin/docs/spec-209-heap-snapshot-procedure.md for the investigation record
- Keep the update concise (< 30 lines added)

Files affected:
- apps/admin/CLAUDE.md
- (read-only reference): apps/admin/docs/spec-209-heap-snapshot-procedure.md

Test requirements: none — documentation task. Verified by reviewer inspection.

subtasks.title: Add Healthcheck section to CLAUDE.md
Add SSR Safety Rules section to CLAUDE.md
Link to procedure document
